response:
In short: the 502s were an Application Gateway→backend connectivity problem, not a WAF regression or server 500s from the App Service.

What we found
- AGWAccessLogs showed 0 HTTP 500s from the backend; the 502s were clustered in short windows (13:46–13:50, 14:14–14:15, 14:38–14:51 UTC).  
- AGW metrics showed HealthyHostCount=1/UnhealthyHostCount=0 while FailedRequests spiked — i.e. short transient failures that didn’t flip backend health.  
- App Service (azure-mcp-prod) was Running, App Insights and App Service metrics show continued Requests and Http2xx and Http5xx=0. show-backend-health reported the backend healthy.  
- AGWFirewallLogs/WAF showed expected AllowTrustedVpnFazureMcp/VpnOnly behavior and did not indicate a WAF regression causing the 502s.

Additional relevant finding
- There was a managed certificate renewal failure for the App Service because DNS for the custom domain pointed at the App Gateway IP instead of the App Service IP. That DNS/cert mismatch is a likely contributor to intermittent TLS/connection failures between AGW and the backend. A manual app restart also occurred during the window.

Bottom line
- The 502s were transient gateway→backend connectivity/TLS/handshake failures (and/or short-lived backend unavailability) that did not originate as application 500 errors or a WAF change. We identified DNS/certificate issues and transient restarts as notable contributing factors.

In AGWAccessLogs for fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com over the prior 24h, there were 0 HTTP 500 responses, multiple 502s concentrated around 13:46-13:50 UTC and 14:14-14:15 UTC and especially 14:38-14:51 UTC, and a small number of 403s. AGWFirewallLogs showed trusted Rackspace/Janus traffic matching AllowTrustedVpnFazureMcp and current 403s were mainly expected VpnOnly blocks from external/scanner or preview IPs such as 146.70.189.237 and 52.123.190.89/125. The fazure-mcp backend is App Service azure-mcp-prod in rg-azure-mcp-prod with linked App Insights azure-mcp-prod. App Service state was Running and App Gateway show-backend-health reported the backend healthy. App Service metrics showed Requests and Http2xx continuing through the window and Http5xx stayed 0 throughout, confirming the backend was not returning server 500s. App Gateway metrics showed HealthyHostCount stayed 1 and UnhealthyHostCount stayed 0 throughout the incident while FailedRequests spiked, indicating short transient backend unavailability that did not trip health-state change

Root cause was that recent POST /mcp failures on fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com at 2026-04-14T17:51:41Z through 2026-04-14T18:06:13Z were blocked by custom rule VpnOnly under policy scope fazure-mcp, even though those IPs are the known JanusAWS set already present in the global policy. Fixed live by updating site policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax-fazure-mcp-sites rules AllowTrustedVpnFazureMcp and VpnOnly, and global policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax rules AllowTrustedVpnFazureOauthMcpAuth and FazureOauthVpnOnly, adding all four Janus AWS IPs to the IPMatch lists.

AGWAccessLogs in workspace ecae2296-e4c2-47f2-9336-24599caea6be showed repeated GET /authorize requests returning 403 between 2026-03-25T14:40:58Z and 2026-03-25T14:41:34Z. AGWFirewallLogs showed custom rule BlockKnownPath blocking the full RequestUri including query string, and managed rule 931130 (Possible Remote File Inclusion / Off-Domain Reference/Link) plus 949110 anomaly score also matched on redirect_uri=http://localhost:6274/oauth/callback/debug and resource=https://fazure-mcp-dev.mscloud.rackspace.com/mcp. Root cause was that custom allow rule AllowTrustedVpnFazureOauthMcpAuth only matched bare paths like ^/(.well-known/oauth-authorization-server|register|authorize(?:/callback)?|token|jwks|oauth2/authorize-internal)$, but App Gateway evaluated RequestUri with query string for /authorize. Fixed live by updating the allow rule on global WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax at priority 5 to regex ^/([.]well-known/oauth-authorizat
[specifics: ip: 72.32.180.181; endpoint: fazure-oauth.mscloud.rackspace.com/authorize; workspace_id: ecae2296-e4c2-47f2-9336-24599caea6be; blocked_rule: BlockKnownPath (blocks full RequestUri including query string); managed_rules: 931130, 949110; redirect_uri: http://localhost:6274/oauth/callback/debug; resource: https://fazure-mcp-dev.mscloud.rackspace.com/mcp; allow_rule_before: ^/(.well-known/oauth-authorization-server|register|authorize(?:/callback)?|token|jwks|oauth2/authorize-internal)$; allow_rule_after: ^/([.]well-known/oauth-authorization-server|register|authorize(/callback)?|token|jwks|oauth2/authorize-internal)([?].*)?$; waf_policy: waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax (Allow rule priority=5, BlockKnownPath priority=6)]", "2026-03-25: On 2026-03-25, a reusable WAF mitigation pattern was established for trusted internal MCP SQL traffic behind Azure Application Gateway. Rackspace VPN IP 72.32.180.181 was receiving 403s on POST /mcp to fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com because the per-listener WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax-fazure-mcp-sites allowed the VPN through the VpnOnly rule but still sent the JSON request body through OWASP managed inspection. AGWFirewallLogs showed SQL injection false positives on ARGS:params.arguments.query with rules 920230, 942100, 942150, 942260, 942360, 942370, 942380, 942410, 942480, then final 949110 anomaly score block. Root cause was that the dedicated site WAF policy copied managed rules but did not include an allow-short-circuit for trusted Rackspace VPN traffic to the MCP endpoint. Fixed live by creating custom allow rule AllowTrustedVpnFazureMcp on listener policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax-fazure-mcp-sites at priority 3, after RateLimit at 1 and VpnOnly at 2. The rule matches Host fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com and later also fazure-mcp-dev.mscloud.rackspace.com, RequestUri beginning /mcp, and Rackspace VPN IP ranges. This short-circuits m
[specifics: waf_policy: waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax-fazure-mcp-sites; custom_allow_rule: AllowTrustedVpnFazureMcp (priority=3); rule_priority_order: RateLimit=1, VpnOnly=2, AllowTrustedVpnFazureMcp=3; mcp_hosts: fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com; fazure-mcp-dev.mscloud.rackspace.com; request_uri_prefix: /mcp; rackspace_vpn_ip: 72.32.180.181; owasp_rule_ids: 920230, 942100, 942150, 942260, 942360, 942370, 942380, 942410, 942480, 949110; agw_log_sqli_field: ARGS:params.arguments.query (SQL injection false positives); transaction: d01bb9fed06bbc7fd2c1e28c031119eb (HTTP 200)]", "2026-03-25: On 2026-03-25, investigated fazure-mcp WAF 403s for POST /mcp from Rackspace VPN IP 72.32.180.181. Local Azure-MCP rollout artifacts showed the per-listener WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax-fazure-mcp-sites was created by copying managedRules and policySettings from the global policy, then attaching only site custom rules RateLimit, VpnOnly, BlockSensitiveFiles, BlockKnownPath, GeoBlocking, and AllowSafeFilters. The global RackspaceVPN Allow rule was not copied into the site policy. Result: trusted VPN traffic still traverses OWASP CRS 3.2 managed rules on /mcp and legitimate MCP SQL request bodies are likely blocked as SQLi false positives. Recommended fix is either a listener-policy custom Allow rule for trusted VPN /mcp traffic or a narrower per-rule exclusion on the JSON arg carrying SQL text after confirming the exact RuleId in AGWFirewallLogs.

Live AGW logs showed OPTIONS /register returned 200, while POST /register was blocked by global WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax with rule 931130 (Possible Remote File Inclusion / Off-Domain Reference/Link) on client_uri=https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/inspector and redirect_uris=http://localhost:6274/oauth/callback/debug, followed by 949110 anomaly score block. Updated global WAF custom rules by moving BlockKnownPath from priority 5 to 6 and adding AllowTrustedVpnFazureOauthMcpAuth at priority 5 for Host=fazure-oauth.mscloud.rackspace.com, RequestUri regex ^/(\.well-known/oauth-authorization-server|register|authorize(?:/callback)?|token|jwks|oauth2/authorize-internal)$, and Rackspace VPN IP ranges. This bypasses managed WAF inspection and the generic authorize/token path blocker for trusted internal MCP OAuth registration/auth flows.
[specifics: client_ip: 72.32.180.178; host: fazure-oauth.mscloud.rackspace.com; waf_policy: waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax; waf_block_rules: 931130, 949110; blocked_request: POST /register blocked; client_uri=https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/inspector; redirect_uris=http://localhost:6274/oauth/callback/debug; options_ok: OPTIONS /register returned 200; BlockKnownPath_priority_change: BlockKnownPath priority 5->6]", "2026-03-19: On 2026-03-19 after expanding the fazure WAF lockdown, a live burst test from Rackspace VPN public IP 72.32.180.181 against https://fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com/mcp did not trigger the Application Gateway WAF RateLimit rule. Baseline responses from the VPN client were /health -> 200 and /mcp -> 401. A 130-request burst to /mcp completed with 130 HTTP 401 responses and the immediate follow-up request also returned 401. Log Analytics confirmed 132 /mcp requests with HttpStatus 401 in the 2026-03-19T19:39:00Z minute for ClientIp 72.32.180.181. AGWFirewallLogs for the same client/host/path only showed Bot Manager matches (rule IDs 300300 and 300700) and no custom rate-limit block events. The WAF policy still shows the RateLimit custom rule enabled at priority 1 with threshold 100, duration OneMin, groupByUserSession ClientAddr, host match values for the four MCP hostnames, RequestUri begins with /mcp, policy mode Prevention, and managed rule set version 3.2 present. This indicates the configured WAF rate-limit rule is still not enforcing in production even after allow-rule reordering.
